generated_comment:
A: Arre, in our house also it has simply evolved that way. I handle the bank work, repairs, outside errands; she manages the kitchen and day-to-day running of the home.
B: Sensible arrangement.
A: Exactly. Jo jahan better ho, wahi kare. Why make it ideological? If I make tea once in a while everyone says, 'wah, what a modern husband,' but frankly the home runs better under her supervision.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames a gendered household division as merely practical and natural rather than unequal. It encodes efficiency framing ('jo jahan better ho'), men handling finances/external tasks, women handling domestic labor, and the 'modern husband' praise for minimal male contribution.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
